EY presenting price Wed AM 3.31.26 KK - interested in demo and POC of spend analysis 3.17.26 KK - fu with EY 3.3.26 KK - argos looking for more information on what a POC would look like - connecting internally 2.27.26 KK - FU with EY on any updates 2.18.26 KK - EY shared data request for implementation and SaaS pricing to grupo argos. opp.ais_why:
• Concrete AI-product buyer interest sits in the next-step note from 3/31/26: "interested in demo and POC of spend analysis". A spend-analysis POC aligns to ML iAnalyze and Merlin analytics. This is the strongest buyer-stated AI signal in this part of the cohort after Teads. The deal is advancing through commercials with four pricing scenarios shared and a business case in progress.
• Products are the broadest in this cohort - Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA, iLogix, iCompliance and ML iAnalyze. But AI importance is 'Neutral', which signals Zycus does not perceive the buyer as AI-driven. An EY-led evaluation, multi-pricing scenarios and a structured business case look more like procurement-rigor buying than AI-vision buying. The POC scope is spend analysis - a discrete use case, not Agentic or Intake.
• Avoma was fully exhausted - zero calls at opportunity or account level, no Grupo Argos contacts in OpportunityContactRole (only EY), and zero on grupoargos.com. The deal is EY-led entirely. The spend-analysis POC quote is real but limited in scope, which holds this at Curious.

sources: Next Step 31 Mar 2026: interested in demo and POC of spend analysis
Next Step 26 May 2026: sharing business case this week with EY (internal artifact, not buyer-validated)
Next Step 12 May 2026: connecting on business case outline with EY team
No Avoma calls for Grupo Argos account or opportunity
No readable Salesforce value/pain fields
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been articulated by Grupo Argos across the 165-day deal lifetime. The Next Step history references a business case outline shared with EY on 12 May and again on 26 May, but this was an internal Zycus-EY artifact, not a buyer-validated value case. No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c) were readable, and no Avoma calls exist to capture buyer-stated pain, savings targets, cycle-time reduction, or cost impact. The only demand signal is EY's 31 Mar note that Argos is interested in a spend analysis demo and POC, which implies procurement visibility or cost-reduction interest but provides no quantified baseline or target. Without a buyer conversation on record, we have no confirmed pain owner, no baseline metrics, and no agreed success criteria. The path forward is to secure a direct Argos stakeholder call and co-develop a quantified value case anchored to their procurement spend, supplier count, cycle times, or compliance gaps.

sources: Zero Salesforce OpportunityContactRole records for Grupo Argos

Next Step 29 Jun 2026: leadership change at Argos (prior sponsor may be out of role)

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed across the 165-day deal. Zero Salesforce contact roles exist for Grupo Argos, so no internal advocate, coach, or influencer is on record. All engagement has been routed through EY as intermediary, and EY has not introduced or named an Argos champion. The Next Step history shows EY acting as the point of contact but provides no evidence of EY advocating for Zycus internally at Argos, sharing competitive intelligence, or driving the buyer's agenda on our behalf. The 16 Jun entry (EY has no update) and 29 Jun entry (leadership change at Argos, election slowdown) suggest EY is passive, not proactive, and may lack deep access or influence at Argos. Without a champion, we have no internal intelligence on decision-maker priorities, no early warning on risks or competitors, and no one pulling us into the evaluation process. The leadership change at Argos (29 Jun) may mean any prior internal sponsor is no longer in role, further weakening our position. Immediate action: demand EY facilitate an introduction to the Argos procurement or operations executive who owns the S2P transformation, assess their willingness to advocate for Zycus, and develop that relationship directly rather than relying on EY as sole conduit.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c field unpopulated
No Next Step entries referencing competitors
No Avoma calls to capture competitive discussion
Next Step 31 Mar 2026: interested in spend analysis demo and POC (may imply comparative evaluation, but unconfirmed)
competition.narrative: No competitors have been identified or discussed across the deal lifecycle. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is unpopulated, and no Next Step entry, task, or Avoma call references another vendor. The 31 Mar interest in a spend analysis POC could imply Argos is evaluating multiple solutions or already has an incumbent, but this is speculative. Without buyer access, we have no visibility into whether Argos is running a competitive RFP, benchmarking Zycus against Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, or another S2P platform, or whether they are sole-sourcing through EY's recommendation. The absence of competition data may reflect the partner-intermediated nature of the deal (EY may not be sharing competitive intelligence) or may indicate the deal is early and Argos has not yet engaged other vendors. Either scenario is risky: if Argos is evaluating competitors and we are unaware, we cannot tailor our positioning or counter their strengths; if the deal is sole-sourced to Zycus but stalled for 165 days, it may not be a real opportunity. Immediate action: in the re-engagement call with Argos, directly ask whether they are evaluating other procurement platforms, what their current state is (incumbent vendor, manual processes, ERP-native tools), and how they are approaching vendor selection.

sources: Next Step 31 Mar 2026: interested in demo and POC of spend analysis (inferred pain: lack of spend visibility)

No Avoma calls to capture buyer-stated pain
identify_pain.narrative: The only pain signal on record is inferred from the 31 Mar Next Step, where EY notes Argos is interested in a demo and POC of spend analysis. This implies Argos lacks procurement spend visibility, which is a common pain point for large industrial conglomerates: inability to aggregate supplier spend, identify savings opportunities, enforce compliance, or benchmark pricing. However, this is entirely inferred; no Argos stakeholder has articulated the pain directly, and no quantified impact (e.g., $Xm in untracked spend, Y% maverick buying, Z-day invoice cycle times, compliance failures) has been stated. The broad product scope (full S2P suite plus Merlin AI) suggests Argos may also be experiencing pain in contract management, supplier onboarding, e-procurement adoption, invoice processing, and risk/compliance management, but these are speculative without buyer confirmation. No Salesforce pain fields are readable, and no Avoma calls exist to capture the pain owner's voice. The 29 Jun note that a leadership change and election have slowed progress may indicate the pain is not urgent or that the new leadership is reprioritizing, which would weaken the compelling event. Immediate action: in the re-engagement call, ask Argos procurement or operations lead to describe the top 2-3 operational or financial problems they are solving for, quantify the cost of inaction, and name the internal pain owner driving the initiative.

sources: Next Step 18 Feb 2026: EY shared data request for implementation and SaaS pricing to Grupo Argos; no data returned
Next Step 20 Apr, 24 Apr, 12 May 2026: commercials and pricing shared with EY, not with Argos directly
No Current_Contract_Expiration__c in Salesforce (field unpopulated)
Zero Salesforce activities or notes referencing MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement terms
No Avoma calls to capture contracting or paper-process discussion
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, or implementation scoping mechanics have been discussed or documented. The Next Step history shows commercials and pricing scenarios were shared with EY in April and May (20 Apr: received commercial inputs, submitted QIT; 24 Apr: four pricing scenarios shared; 12 May: updated commercials shared), but there is no record of Argos reviewing an MSA, SOW, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement terms. The 18 Feb entry notes EY shared a data request for implementation and SaaS pricing inputs to Argos, which implies readiness to scope an implementation, but no data was returned and no implementation planning followed. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c is populated, so there is no forcing-function contract renewal date. With zero direct buyer engagement and no legal or procurement contacts identified, we have no visibility into Argos's contracting authority (procurement vs legal), whether they require an RFP response before contracting, whether EY will paper the deal as a subcontract or Argos will contract directly with Zycus, or what Argos's InfoSec, data residency, and integration (ERP, existing procurement systems) requirements are. The 165-day silence and lack of implementation data suggest paper process is not yet engaged. Immediate action: in the re-engagement call with Argos, surface the contracting path, identify their legal and procurement leads, and confirm whether EY is the contracting party or an implementation partner only.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged across the 165-day deal. No Salesforce contact roles exist for Grupo Argos, meaning no personnel are mapped at all, let alone a budget authority. The Next Step history on 18 Feb references EY sharing a data request for implementation and SaaS pricing to Grupo Argos, implying EY acted as intermediary to a buyer stakeholder, but that person is unnamed and their authority level (procurement lead, CFO, VP Operations) is unknown. No subsequent Next Step or activity names who at Argos received the pricing, reviewed it, or has approval authority. The partner (EY) has provided no update since 16 Jun, and the 29 Jun note cites a leadership change at Argos, which may mean the prior point of contact is no longer in role or the initiative has stalled pending new leadership priorities. Without direct access to Argos and no named stakeholders on record, we cannot validate who controls the 350k budget, whether it is allocated, or whether the new leadership supports the investment. Immediate action: demand EY introduce Zycus directly to the Argos procurement executive or CFO sponsor to confirm budget authority and strategic alignment.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is entirely opaque. All interactions have been partner-mediated through EY, with no direct Argos participation visible in Salesforce or Avoma. The Next Step history shows EY acting as intermediary: EY shared a data request to Argos (18 Feb), EY was to present pricing to Argos (20 Apr), EY rescheduled calls (24 Apr), and EY provided updates or no-updates across the timeline. No Argos personnel have joined a call, responded to outreach, or been named in a contact role, so we have no read on their internal approval chain, procurement process, or decision timeline. The 29 Jun note that Argos leadership has changed and the election has slowed things suggests the process may be on hold or under review by new stakeholders, but we have no confirmation of whether the initiative is still prioritized, who the new decision authority is, or what the revised timeline looks like. The lack of a qualified_date or any stage-date progression (no Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c, no Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c captured) means we have not validated that Argos is running a structured procurement or that Zycus is formally in their vendor pipeline. Immediate action: secure a direct meeting with Argos procurement or the executive sponsor to map the approval process, confirm stakeholders, and establish a mutual action plan with dates.

sources: Next Step 31 Mar 2026: interested in demo and POC of spend analysis
Next Step 3 Mar 2026: Argos looking for more information on what a POC would look like

decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria have been captured from the buyer. The 31 Mar Next Step notes Argos interest in a spend analysis demo and POC, and the 3 Mar entry states Argos is looking for more information on what a POC would look like, but these are early discovery signals, not structured evaluation criteria. No RFP, RFI, business requirements document, or scorecard has been referenced across the Next Step history or any Salesforce field. The deal has not progressed to demos, workshops, or use-case validation sessions where criteria would typically emerge. The full S2P product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, Merlin, iLogix, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, iCompliance, ANA) suggests a comprehensive Source-to-Pay transformation, yet we have no record of which modules are must-haves, which are nice-to-haves, how Argos will score AI/Merlin capabilities, or what technical, compliance, or integration requirements they prioritize. EY as intermediary has not relayed buyer criteria. Without direct buyer access, we cannot validate fit or tailor the POC to the decision framework. The move is to insist on a joint discovery workshop with Argos procurement and IT to document their evaluation rubric and top 3-5 priorities.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 15 Jan 2026 and has remained in Formal Evaluation stage for 165 days with zero buyer engagement. All movement is rep-to-partner (EY) touches recorded in Next Step; no Argos personnel have been contacted, no Salesforce activities logged, and no Avoma calls held. The timeline shows initial discovery interest (Feb-Mar), commercial preparation (Apr-May), and a June stall with EY reporting no update (16 Jun) and citing Argos leadership change plus election slowdown (29 Jun). No stage progression, no field-history updates to Amount or CloseDate, and no qualified_date or submission-date milestones captured.

Deal is entirely partner-intermediated through EY with no direct Grupo Argos access. EY has provided no update since 16 Jun and reported Argos leadership change and election slowdown 29 Jun, signaling EY may lack deep access or influence at Argos. Partner passivity is blocking buyer engagement and validation.

Argos leadership change (29 Jun) may mean prior stakeholders are out of role or initiative is under review by new leadership. No Economic Buyer or executive sponsor identified, so we cannot validate whether new leadership supports the S2P transformation or has reprioritized budget.

EY cited recent election and political environment as factor slowing Argos progress (29 Jun). If Argos is in a country with election-driven policy uncertainty, procurement transformation may be on hold pending new government priorities or budget allocation.

No validation that opportunity is still live. Zero buyer engagement for 165 days, no demos or POC launched, no stakeholders mapped, and partner has no forward motion. Deal may be dead or deprioritized but not yet marked Closed Lost.

champion_strength.source: Zero OpportunityContactRole records; Next Step 29 Jun 2026 EY reported leadership change at Argos
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified or developed. Zero Salesforce contact roles for Grupo Argos, and EY as partner intermediary has not introduced or named an internal advocate. Leadership change at Argos (29 Jun) means any prior sponsor may be out of role.

items.commitment: Provide implementation data and SaaS pricing inputs requested by EY

items.action: Demand EY facilitate a direct video call with the Grupo Argos procurement or operations executive who owns the S2P transformation within 7 days. Agenda: validate the initiative is still live post-leadership-change, confirm the new stakeholder and their priorities, assess AI/Merlin appetite, and secure a follow-on workshop to scope the spend analysis POC. If EY cannot deliver the meeting, escalate to the EY partner relationship owner to unlock access or assess whether to pause the opportunity.
items.trigger: EY reported Argos leadership change and election slowdown; no buyer engagement for 165 days; partner has no update and no forward meeting scheduled
items.expected_effect: Re-engages the buyer, validates whether the deal is live, maps new stakeholders post-leadership-change, and creates a path to POC scoping. If Argos is no longer prioritizing the initiative, allows us to qualify out and reallocate resources rather than carrying a ghost deal to Dec close date.

items.action: If Argos re-engagement call confirms the initiative is live, map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker in the first 10 minutes: ask who controls the budget, who approves the investment, and what the approval process looks like. Secure their agreement to join the next commercial or ROI discussion. Add them to Salesforce as contact roles within 24 hours of the call.
items.trigger: Zero Salesforce contact roles for Grupo Argos; no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified across 165 days; $350k deal with no budget authority mapped
items.expected_effect: Establishes who has commercial authority and can approve the deal, enabling us to validate forecast defensibility and tailor the business case and pricing to their priorities. Without EB/DM mapped, the deal cannot progress to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation.

items.action: Co-develop a quantified business case with the Argos procurement lead on the re-engagement call. Ask them to share current-state pain: procurement spend under management, supplier count, cycle times for sourcing/contracting/PO-to-invoice, maverick buying rate, and compliance gaps. Work backward from their pain to build a 3-year ROI model showing cost savings, efficiency gains, and risk reduction, and commit to delivering a one-page executive summary within 5 business days of the call for them to share with their CFO or new leadership.
items.trigger: No quantified business case validated with Argos; only internal Zycus-EY business case outline exists; no buyer-stated metrics or ROI targets on record
items.expected_effect: Creates a buyer-validated, quantified value case that Argos can use to justify the investment to new leadership and secure budget approval. Without metrics, the deal is a capability pitch, not a business decision.

items.action: Launch the spend analysis POC as the validation gate to the relevant stakeholder stage. Scope it tightly: 4-week timeline, 3 use cases (spend visibility, supplier consolidation, savings opportunity ID), and 2-3 Argos stakeholders engaged (procurement ops, analytics, and one business-unit buyer). Deliver a readout with quantified findings and a roadmap to full S2P deployment. Secure a POC kickoff meeting within 14 days of the re-engagement call.
items.trigger: Argos requested POC scoping information 3 Mar; expressed interest in spend analysis demo and POC 31 Mar; no POC launched across 165 days
items.expected_effect: Provides hands-on validation of Zycus value, creates tangible results Argos can show new leadership, and advances the deal from the relevant stakeholder to the relevant stakeholder is the forcing function to multi-thread and map stakeholders.

items.action: Position Merlin Agentic AI as the differentiation wedge in the POC readout and commercial discussion. Show how Merlin Intake auto-triages procurement requests, how Merlin negotiation agent accelerates supplier pricing rounds, and how Merlin contract intelligence surfaces risk and savings in legacy agreements. Tie it to the relevant stakeholder appetite (if validated on the re-engagement call) or frame it as future-proofing their S2P stack. Deliver a Merlin-specific demo or workshop within 3 weeks of POC kickoff.
items.trigger: Products scope includes Merlin and Agentic AI, but no AI positioning or buyer validation across 165 days; no AIS score populated; leadership change at Argos may create opportunity to reset narrative with new stakeholders
items.expected_effect: Differentiates Zycus from traditional S2P competitors (Coupa, Ariba) who lack agentic AI, and positions the deal as a strategic AI-driven transformation rather than a system replacement. If new Argos leadership is AI-forward, Merlin becomes the champion-building wedge.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 15 Jan 2026, still in Formal Evaluation 165 days later with no qualified_date or submission-date progression captured. LastActivityDate 15 Jan (creation day) means zero documented buyer engagement across the entire 165-day lifetime. Close date 31 Dec 2026 (185 days out) implies a 350-day cycle, but with zero validation milestones hit and no live buyer thread, the forecast is indefensible. Partner (EY) reported no update 16 Jun and cited Argos leadership change plus election slowdown 29 Jun, but no forward buyer meeting is scheduled. Time-in-stage: 165 days in Formal Evaluation with no demos, no POC launched, no proposal, no pricing presented to the buyer (only to EY). Required pace to close by Dec: immediate buyer re-engagement, EB/DM mapping, POC kickoff, and structured validation within 60 days, yet the deal has been silent since creation.

north_star_verdict.headline: Partner-intermediated deal dark for 165 days with zero buyer access, no Economic Buyer or champion mapped, and leadership change at Argos stalling progress; close date 185 days away is indefensible without immediate re-engagement

flags: PARTNER-ONLY RELATIONSHIP IS A CRITICAL VULNERABILITY: This deal has zero direct Grupo Argos contacts mapped and zero buyer calls. The sole relationship is through EY, who reported no update on 16 Jun (10 days ago). Partner-intermediated deals with no direct buyer champion or EB access are extremely fragile — we have no control, no intel, and no protection if the partner deprioritizes or the buyer stalls. The immediate move is to demand direct introductions to the Argos evaluation team and Economic Buyer, and to run a joint discovery call with EY + Argos where we can validate the opportunity, map stakeholders, and establish our own relationship. If EY cannot or will not facilitate direct access within 7 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager to engage EY leadership or disqualify the opportunity.

162 DAYS IN FORMAL EVALUATION WITH the relevant stakeholder: The deal has been in Formal Evaluation since creation (15 Jan, 162 days ago) but there is no evidence of a formal evaluation happening — no demos delivered, no POC executed, no RFI/RFP response, no buyer validation sessions. The Next Step history shows Argos expressed interest in a spend analysis demo and POC on 31 Mar (87 days ago) but neither was delivered. A deal that sits in Formal Evaluation for 5+ months with no buyer-facing milestones is stalled or dead. The re-engagement move must include a concrete near-term validation event (a discovery call, a demo, or a scoping workshop) scheduled within 14 days to prove the deal is still live.

NO ECONOMIC BUYER OR DECISION MAKER MAPPED: With zero buyer contact roles and zero calls, we have no visibility into who at Grupo Argos controls the $350k budget, who signs off, or who drives the decision. Commercials with four pricing scenarios were submitted to EY on 20 Apr (67 days ago) but we do not know if they reached the EB or what the reaction was. Mapping the EB and running a direct commercial conversation with them is a prerequisite to any realistic forecast or close plan. This must be a top-3 action in the next 14 days.

hard.ais_why:
• Concrete AI-product buyer interest sits in the next-step note from 3/31/26: "interested in demo and POC of spend analysis". A spend-analysis POC aligns to ML iAnalyze and Merlin analytics. This is the strongest buyer-stated AI signal in this part of the cohort after Teads. The deal is advancing through commercials with four pricing scenarios shared and a business case in progress.
• Products are the broadest in this cohort - Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA, iLogix, iCompliance and ML iAnalyze. But AI importance is 'Neutral', which signals Zycus does not perceive the buyer as AI-driven. An EY-led evaluation, multi-pricing scenarios and a structured business case look more like procurement-rigor buying than AI-vision buying. The POC scope is spend analysis - a discrete use case, not Agentic or Intake.
• Avoma was fully exhausted - zero calls at opportunity or account level, no Grupo Argos contacts in OpportunityContactRole (only EY), and zero on grupoargos.com. The deal is EY-led entirely. The spend-analysis POC quote is real but limited in scope, which holds this at Curious.

Provide information on what a spend analysis POC would look like
Partner-intermediated deal with no direct buyer access; EY reported no update 10 days ago and has no forward motion
No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified; zero buyer contact roles; no visibility into who controls budget or approves the investment
Deal has been dark for 162 days since creation; no buyer engagement; partner has no update; no compelling event or buyer-driven timeline visible
Zero buyer stakeholders mapped; single-threaded through a partner who has gone quiet; no champion, no multi-threading, no internal advocate at Argos
No validation that the opportunity is still live; no demos, no POC, no buyer engagement since creation; may be dead

PARTNER-ONLY RELATIONSHIP IS A CRITICAL VULNERABILITY: This deal has zero direct Grupo Argos contacts mapped and zero buyer calls. The sole relationship is through EY, who reported no update on 16 Jun (10 days ago). Partner-intermediated deals with no direct buyer champion or EB access are extremely fragile — we have no control, no intel, and no protection if the partner deprioritizes or the buyer stalls. The immediate move is to demand direct introductions to the Argos evaluation team and Economic Buyer, and to run a joint discovery call with EY + Argos where we can validate the opportunity, map stakeholders, and establish our own relationship. If EY cannot or will not facilitate direct access within 7 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager to engage EY leadership or disqualify the opportunity.

162 DAYS IN FORMAL EVALUATION WITH the relevant stakeholder: The deal has been in Formal Evaluation since creation (15 Jan, 162 days ago) but there is no evidence of a formal evaluation happening — no demos delivered, no POC executed, no RFI/RFP response, no buyer validation sessions. The Next Step history shows Argos expressed interest in a spend analysis demo and POC on 31 Mar (87 days ago) but neither was delivered. A deal that sits in Formal Evaluation for 5+ months with no buyer-facing milestones is stalled or dead. The re-engagement move must include a concrete near-term validation event (a discovery call, a demo, or a scoping workshop) scheduled within 14 days to prove the deal is still live.

NO ECONOMIC BUYER OR DECISION MAKER MAPPED: With zero buyer contact roles and zero calls, we have no visibility into who at Grupo Argos controls the $350k budget, who signs off, or who drives the decision. Commercials with four pricing scenarios were submitted to EY on 20 Apr (67 days ago) but we do not know if they reached the EB or what the reaction was. Mapping the EB and running a direct commercial conversation with them is a prerequisite to any realistic forecast or close plan. This must be a top-3 action in the next 14 days.
